_doc: GR00T-SONIC demo flows. A flow is an ordered list of segments played as one looping live session (modulo loop, no GUI/server restart). Each segment: motion (one of dance|lunge|macarena|kick|squat|jump|walk), steps (control steps @50Hz, ~0.02s each), and optional full_bootstrap (replay the whole segment from the open-loop dump token instead of handing to live GR00T after the bootstrap window — used to make a one-shot locomotion clip play continuously, e.g. a walk-through between actions). Self-sustaining motions (squat/lunge/dance/macarena) run pure live; one-shot motions (kick/walk/jump) get a bootstrap window by default, or full_bootstrap for continuous playback.

flow1.desc: Discrete actions, equal time, looping. All self-sustaining (pure live GR00T, no bootstrap).

flow2.desc: Action -> walk-through -> action -> walk-through ... interleaved locomotion, looping. Actions are live GR00T; walk segments are continuous (full_bootstrap replay of the walk-and-turn clip).

flow3.desc: flow3 LAFAN hand-picked windows, user order fight1->run3->fight2->run2->dance1->run1, looping. LIVE GR00T (outputs/gr00t_sonic_flow3): each window starts mid-action (one-shot) so it gets a short bootstrap to ENTER the move, then hands to live GR00T inference. One-shot windows may settle toward standing after entry (the known no-memory limitation); the offline gear_sonic_flow3.sh loop is the guaranteed-smooth version. Add full_bootstrap:true to a segment to replay it whole (continuous, but not live). Requires GR00T_CKPT=outputs/gr00t_sonic_flow3/checkpoint-6000 PKL=data/seg_flow3_all.pkl PRED_DIR=datasets/sonic_vla_pred_flow3.
